diagnosis:
The failure is not explained by the current `factory/routes.yaml` static caps alone. In current HEAD, `factory/routes.yaml` routes `sm` to `azure/gpt-5.4` under the active `default_provider: azure`, with `model_limits.azure/gpt-5.4.max_output_tokens: 16384`, and only the direct fallback route maps `sm` to `deepseek/deepseek-chat` with an 8192 cap. But the concern evidence shows the failing run was actually `persona=sm ... model=deepseek/deepseek-chat ... error="json parse failed at max_tokens=65536 finish_reason=length"`. That combination is internally inconsistent with the checked-in routing table: neither the active Azure route nor the DeepSeek model limit in this file should yield `max_tokens=65536`.

That mismatch points to a runtime path outside the supplied source bundle: either the chain/runtime is overriding the provider or model, or the request builder is deriving `max_tokens` from some other default/config path not shown here. Because the observed failure mechanism is "truncated JSON at output cap", the true fix may be in dispatch/runtime code that selects provider/model or computes the token cap, not in the persona prompt. The prior failed attempts reinforce this: one tried increasing a Sonnet model limit in `routes.yaml`, which does not match the observed failing model; the other rerouted Azure `sm` to Claude, which also does not address why actual runs are still on DeepSeek with a 65536 cap.

I cannot produce a reliable single-file `factory/routes.yaml` patch from the provided sources without guessing. Any settings-only patch here would repeat the same class of mistake as the abandoned/test-failed attempts: changing static routes or model caps that do not line up with the runtime evidence. To fix this safely, the next investigation needs the dispatch/runtime source that resolves persona -> provider/model and assigns `max_tokens`, plus the tests that cover route resolution and request construction.

proposal.rationale: No safe settings-only patch is justified from the provided files because the observed failing runtime (`deepseek/deepseek-chat` at `max_tokens=65536`) contradicts current HEAD `factory/routes.yaml`. A reliable fix needs the missing dispatch/runtime code path that overrides routing or token caps; changing `routes.yaml` again would likely repeat the prior rejected attempts.
proposal.verification: Inspect and test the runtime model-resolution and request-construction path before any patch: route-resolution smoke test plus the pytest target covering text_run/LiteLLM request config.

escalation_reason: The provided source bundle does not include the runtime code that maps `sm` to the observed `deepseek/deepseek-chat` call or the code that set `max_tokens=65536`, and prior L4 attempts already failed by editing `factory/routes.yaml` directly. Additional context is needed from `factory/chain/` or the request builder to identify the real override path and produce a non-speculative fix.
